# Projects Page Pattern

Model this page on the reference site's project index: simultaneously browsable and sortable.

Purpose:

- show what the person has built
- support both quick scanning and deeper exploration

Reference structure:

- page title and framing statement
- search field
- optional type filter
- grid view for visual browsing
- toggle between grid and table view
- cards or rows with title, short description, type, and date
- pagination when the set is large

Design cues:

- balance polish with evidence of real work
- avoid equal-looking cards with no hierarchy
- reserve images for projects that actually benefit from them
- keep the control row light and utility-first

Good dynamic behaviors:

- search
- filtering
- grid and table toggle
- card hover lift
- sortable table columns
- detail pages for deeper case studies

Keep it profile-agnostic:

- do not assume every project has screenshots
- do not assume startup products, client work, or open-source only
- use table mode when the user has many projects or sparse visuals
